LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HEALTHRIGHT 360, A NONPROFIT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS IN PROGRESS IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SUBSTITUTE A DOE DEFENDANT FOR HEALTHRIGHT 360. HEALTHRIGHT 360 WAS NOT PROPERLY ADDED AS A PARTY, HOWEVER PLAINTIFF JANET PLANELLS, ACTING IN PROPRIA PERSONA AT THE TIME OF FILING HER INITIAL COMPLAINT HAD NO KNOWLEDGE THAT THE "JAIL PSYCHIATRIC SERVICES" ALSO KNOWN AS JPS WAS NOT AN ENTITY BUT A PROGRAM OF THE ENTITY HEALTHRIGHT 360 IN 2014. JPS IS STILL A PROGRAM OF HEALTHRIGHT 360; AND HEALTHRIGHT 360 IS IN CONTRACT WITH THE SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS TO PROVIDE THE JPS PROGRAM AND WAS IN CONTRACT WITH THE BOARD IN 2014 AS WELL. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. Defendant Healthright 360'S Demurrer To Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

In his tentative ruling, Judge Ulmer disclosed that he has made charitable contributions to HealthRIGHT 360 but does not believe that this is a basis for recusal per CCP 170.1. There was no objection by any party.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant HealthRIGHT 360's demurrer to the Third Amended Complaint is OVERRULED. Judge Dekreon already concluded that there was good cause and genuine ignorance to allow the Doe amendment and the court will not revisit that issue. HealthRIGHT 360's authorities are distinguishable and it fails to show that plaintiff's claims are time barred. Scherzer v. Mark (1976) 64 Cal.App.3d 834 stands for the unremarkable proposition that a plaintiff cannot feign ignorance to take advantage of the Doe amendment procedure of CCP 474. In Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sparks Const., Inc. (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1135, the court held that a Doe amendment is improper where the operative pleading has no such allegations. In this case, plaintiff's complaints consistently contained Doe allegations.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by plaintiff's counsel by email in compliance with the tentative ruling. [One order signed for both demurrer and motion to strike.]

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Defendant Healthright 360'S Motion To Strike Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

In his tentative ruling, Judge Ulmer disclosed that he has made charitable contributions to HealthRIGHT 360 but does not believe that this is a basis for recusal per CCP 170.1. There was no objection by any party.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant HealthRIGHT 360's motion to strike is DENIED in its entirety. Causes of action 1 and 2 are not duplicative. Paragraphs 16-17 of the Third Amended Complaint arguably allege that the mistreatment occurred pre and post-conviction and judicially noticeable documents do not indisputably demonstrate that the claims are duplicative. Punitive damages are recoverable on a survivorship claim pursuant to CCP 377.34. Paragraph 1 of the Third Amended Complaint alleges that plaintiff is decedent's successor in interest and brings a survivorship claim. The court liberally reads the complaint as a whole pursuant to CCP 452 and finds that plaintiff pleads both a wrongful death and survivorship claim. Paragraphs 16 and 18-19 of the Third Amended Complaint allege that decedent was mistreated weeks and months prior to the suicide. There is a factual issue if decedent has a cognizable survivor claim that can support the claim for punitives. The court denies HealthRIGHT's motion to strike argument that the Doe amendment was improper based on the reasons adduced in the demurrer.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by plaintiff's counsel by email in compliance with the tentative ruling. [One order signed for both demurrer and motion to strike.]

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with sixty days leave to amend to give Ms. Planells an opportunity to specify what claims she is alleging against which defendants and based on what acts or omissions.
